body.visualDesc: Wide cinematic shot under the Big Oak at dusk. CRITICAL: EXACTLY THREE small chicks are in frame, frozen in a ring around the golden straw nest that holds the pale-cream egg. Chick 1 = Pip: cream-white, wearing a straw farmer hat and red neck bandana, her wing hovering mid-air above the shell, eyes wide, breath held. Chick 2 = Mo: blue-grey, red knitted scarf, tall red comb, sitting perfectly still with wings folded. Chick 3 = Bo: tan sandy-brown, round thin wire-framed eyeglasses on her beak and a green neck scarf, both wings pressed to her own cheeks in suspense. The eggshell is CLOSED with two thin hairline cracks and a faint warm glow through the cracks only - NOT open. No other animals, no lamps on the ground, no extra eggs. Golden-amber dusk light through the oak leaves, first fireflies glowing. The world holds its breath.
